briefdescription.para: Generic Type for adaptor signals.
SceneHolder created signal type.
Locale changed signal type.
Surface size type.
Implementation object.
Create a new adaptor using the window.

simplesect.para: a reference to the adaptor handle

briefdescription.para: Create a new adaptor using render surface.

simplesect.para: a reference to the adaptor handle

briefdescription.para: Create a new adaptor using the SceneHolder.

simplesect.para: a reference to the adaptor handle

briefdescription.para: Create a new adaptor using render surface.

simplesect.para: a reference to the adaptor handle

briefdescription.para: Returns a reference to the instance of the adaptor used by the current thread.

simplesect.para: A reference to the adaptor.
The adaptor has been initialised.
This is only valid in the main thread.

briefdescription.para: Checks whether the adaptor is available.

simplesect.para: true, if it is available, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Virtual Destructor.
Starts the Adaptor.
Pauses the Adaptor.
Resumes the Adaptor, if previously paused.

simplesect.para: If the adaptor is not paused, this does not do anything.

briefdescription.para: Stops the Adaptor.
Ensures that the function passed in is called from the main loop when it is idle.

detaileddescription.para: boolMyFunction(); This callback will be called repeatedly as long as it returns true. A return of 0 deletes this callback.

parameterdescription.para: Sould be set to true if the callback function has a return value.

simplesect.para: true if added successfully, false otherwise
Ownership of the callback is passed onto this class.

briefdescription.para: Adds a new Window instance to the Adaptor.
Removes a previously added callback.

detaileddescription.para:
Function must be called from the main event thread only.
Does nothing if the callback doesn't exist.

briefdescription.para: Processes the idle callbacks.

simplesect.para: This function is intended to be used in the case there is no instance of a Dali::Application i.e. DALi is used in a implementation of a plugin of an application.

briefdescription.para: Replaces the rendering surface.

parameterdescription.para: The window to replace the surface for

briefdescription.para: Replaces the rendering surface.

parameterdescription.para: The SceneHolder to replace the surface for

briefdescription.para: Get the render surface the adaptor is using to render to.

simplesect.para: reference to current render surface

briefdescription.para: Gets native window handle.

simplesect.para: Native window handle

briefdescription.para: Retrieve native window handle that the given actor is added to.

simplesect.para: native window handle

briefdescription.para: Get the native display associated with the graphics backend.

simplesect.para: A handle to the native display

briefdescription.para: Release any locks the surface may hold.

detaileddescription.para: For example, after compositing an offscreen surface, use this method to allow rendering to continue.

briefdescription.para: Set the number of frames per render.

detaileddescription.para:
This enables an application to deliberately render with a reduced FPS.
numberOfVSyncsPerRender
The number of vsyncs between successive renders. Suggest this is a power of two: 1 - render each vsync frame 2 - render every other vsync frame 4 - render every fourth vsync frame 8 - render every eighth vsync frame

briefdescription.para: Set the maximum value of frames per seconds.

simplesect.para: Each frame will render multiple of given maximum render frame rate. For example, if maximumRenderFrameRate = 50.0f, each elapse time could be 20ms, 40ms, 60ms, and so on.

parameterdescription.para: The maximum fps for this adaptor system.

briefdescription.para: The callback is called from the Update/Render thread prior to rendering.

detaileddescription.para:
callback
The function to call
The function is called from the Update thread, so should do as little processing as possible. It is not possible to call any DALi event side APIs from within the callback; doing so will cause instability. Only 1 callback is supported. Setting the callback to NULL will remove the current callback.
A callback of the following type should be used: boolMyFunction(); This callback will be called repeatedly as long as it returns true. A return of 0 deletes this callback.

briefdescription.para: Call this method to notify Dali when scene is created and initialized.

detaileddescription.para: Notify Adaptor that the scene has been created.

briefdescription.para: Call this method to notify Dali when the system language changes.

detaileddescription.para: Use this only when NOT using Dali::Application, As Application created using Dali::Application will automatically receive notification of language change. When Dali::Application is not used, the application developer should use app-core to receive language change notifications and should update Dali by calling this method.

briefdescription.para: Feed a touch point to the adaptor.
Feed a wheel event to the adaptor.
Feed a key event to the adaptor.

parameterdescription.para: The key event holding the key information.

briefdescription.para: Informs core the surface size has changed.
Informs ThreadController the surface size has changed.
Update once. It will not wake up the update thread if we're paused.

simplesect.para: Will not work if the window is hidden.

briefdescription.para: Renders once more even if we're paused.
Relayout and ensure all pending operations are flushed to the update thread.
Requests a future call to Dali::Integration::Core::ProcessEvents(), when the application is idle.
The log factory allows installation of a logger function in worker threads.

simplesect.para: An interface to a logging factory

briefdescription.para: The trace factory allows installation of a trace function in worker threads.

simplesect.para: An interface to a tracing factory

briefdescription.para: Register a processor implementing the Integration::Processor interface with dali-core.

parameterdescription.para: set this processor required to be called after size negotiation. Default is false.

simplesect.para: using this api does not maintain the processor's lifecycle, must be done elsewhere.

briefdescription.para: Unregister a previously registered processor from dali-core.

parameterdescription.para: True if the processor to be unregister is for post processor.

briefdescription.para: Register a processor implementing the Integration::Processor interface with dali-core.

parameterdescription.para: set this processor required to be called after size negotiation. Default is false.

simplesect.para: registered processor will be unregistered automatically after process.

briefdescription.para: Unregister a previously registered once processor from dali-core.

parameterdescription.para: True if the processor to be unregister is for post processor.

briefdescription.para: Get the list of windows created.

simplesect.para: The list of windows

briefdescription.para: Get the list of scene holders.

simplesect.para: The list of scene holers

briefdescription.para: Gets the Object registry.

simplesect.para: The object registry

briefdescription.para: Called when the window becomes fully or partially visible.
Called when the window is fully hidden.
Gets the render thread ID of DALi.

simplesect.para: The render thread ID.
Returns a valid thread ID only if the render thread has started and the system supports thread ID retrieval. Otherwise, returns 0.

briefdescription.para: Gets the UI thread ID of DALi.

simplesect.para: The UI thread ID.
Returns a valid thread ID only if the adaptor has been initialized and the system supports thread ID retrieval. Otherwise, returns 0.

briefdescription.para: Gets the main thread ID of DALi.

simplesect.para: The main thread ID.
Returns a valid thread ID only if the system supports thread ID retrieval. Otherwise, returns 0.

briefdescription.para: The user should connect to this signal if they need to perform any special activities when the surface Dali is being rendered on is resized.

simplesect.para: The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when the language is changed on the device.

simplesect.para: The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when a new window (scene holder) is created.

simplesect.para: The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when the system locale is changed.

simplesect.para: The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: Create an uninitialized Adaptor.
An Adaptor object is used to initialize and control how Dali runs.

detaileddescription.para: It provides a lifecycle interface that allows the application writer to provide their own main loop and other platform related features.
The Adaptor class provides a means for initialising the resources required by the Dali::Core.
When dealing with platform events, the application writer MUST ensure that Dali is called in a thread-safe manner.

As soon as the Adaptor class is created and started, the application writer can initialise their Dali::Actor objects straight away or as required by the main loop they intend to use (there is no need to wait for an initialise signal as per the Dali::Application class).
The Adaptor does emit a Resize signal which informs the user when the surface is resized. Tizen and Linux Adaptors should follow the example below:
If required, you can also connect class member functions to a signal:

simplesect.para: RenderSurface
